And maintaining your ethics. I don't want you lying and cheating to do that, but it's all about closing. You've got to be pit bulls. I don't mean pit bulls in the sense that you're attacking people, but your whole mindset is like, how do I get in touch with anyone who owns a business or manages sales for us? Now, I'll tell you what I would be doing if I were you. I'd be making a list of everyone who owns a business or everyone I know. I'd be using my warm market and I'd be calling every single person I could possibly think of who to who you don't manage your sales or owns a business. I would never do that in risk and relationship with someone unless I knew for a thing. You guys know I'll break this program as you see from the website. There's no reason you guys should not be speaking to every single person that you know. If you want to really succeed as a salesperson, you've got to eat, sleep, and breathe what you're selling. It's got to be like coming at you when you're at a bar, you're getting leads, when you're walking into a hair shop, you're getting leads. You know, when I was in the mortgage industry, I came up with the whole idea of going door to door and I did it really. Well, I had days I made $100,000 going door to door one day when the refar with rates just dropped, right? I came up with the idea because one day I was getting pizza. I was getting a slice of pizza in New York and I saw the guy who was behind the thing. I figured he was the owner. I said, hey, you own a house? He's like, yeah, I said, what rate you paying? He goes, about nine? I'm like, really? I could save you a ton of money. A mortgage broker in the area. And I closed the guy. Getting a slice of pizza. I would have guys. It's the true story. I promise you, I'm not even exaggerating one bit. I can't tell you how many times guys are going door to door and they come back and they say, oh, smashed. This is the area smashed because I had so many guys going door to door that like, you know, they're like, everybody's heard the pitch before. So I said, follow me. And I literally take the guy outside my business and walk across the street. And no one in the picture right across the street. It was almost a guarantee. And so I did this. The first time I did this, it's a true story. I worked on the address was 375 Jericho Turnpike. It was on a main thoroughfare in the town of Saeasa. Big area, right? I was with the corner like this old sort of house that'd be converted into like, it was like a real estate agency that was like on the corner, right? The standalone business.
